news.t: Schwab Ramps Up Competition With 4 New ETF Fee Cuts
Schwab Asset Management Reduces Fees on Four Equity Index ETFs
Active Mutual Funds Owning at Least 1 ETF More Than Doubled
Schwab Asset Management Announces ETF Share Splits
Schwab US Small-Cap (SCHA) ETF: Ripe for a bearish breakout
Small-cap ETFs take a big step higher
Small-cap ETFs keep the rally going
Small-cap and midcap ETFs charge higher as investors continue to rotate

holdings.infoBox: SCHA is an equity ETF with a total of 1705 individual holdings. The top holdings are Sandisk stock at 6.02%, Lumentum Holdings at 1.36%, Revolution Medicines at 0.62%, ATI Inc. at 0.56%, and Sterling Infrastructure at 0.54%.

overview.description: The Schwab U.S. Small-Cap ETF (SCHA) is an exchange-traded fund that mostly invests in small-cap stocks. The fund tracks a market-cap-weighted index of US stocks ranked 751-2,500 by market cap. SCHA was launched on Nov 3, 2009 and is issued by Charles Schwab.
infoTable: Dow Jones US Total Stock Market Small-Cap

data.title: Schwab US Small-Cap (SCHA) ETF: Ripe for a bearish breakout
data.text: The Schwab US Small-Cap ETF (SCHA) stock has been in a consolidation phase since July. It was trading at $40 on Friday, down by more than 25% from the highest level this year.

data.text: Small-cap exchange-traded funds outperformed Wednesday on a strong day for the broad markets. The iShares Russell 2000 ETF gained 2% late morning, as did the SPDR Portfolio S&P 600 Small Cap ETF.

data.title: The state of the ETF market
data.text: ETFs are gaining popularity, and major Wall Street firms are seizing on the trend. Tony Davidow, VP of Alternative Beta & Asset Allocation Strategist at Charles Schwab, discusses the shift.
